text.title: rust-testing-integration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the rust-testing-integration methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot to drive Axum routers in-process; never bind a real listener on a fixed port.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded ports cause parallel-test port collisions; oneshot drives the router without any socket allocation.

rule.statement: Use testcontainers-rs for any integration test needing a real database. Per-test schema (CREATE SCHEMA test_<uuid>) or per-test transaction rollback isolates state.
rule.rationale: Shared containers without isolation fail in random CI order — one test's INSERT becomes another's SELECT.

rule.statement: Run migrations (sqlx migrate run / diesel migration run) inside test setup before any assertions.
rule.rationale: Freshly-started containers have no schema; skipping migrations gives confusing 'relation does not exist' errors.

rule.statement: Use insta for snapshot tests of structured output longer than ~10 lines. Do not hand-write multi-line assert_eq! strings.
rule.rationale: Hand-written expected strings break on whitespace/ordering changes; insta stores .snap files git tracks cleanly.

rule.statement: Specify #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)] for integration tests that spawn tasks or hit HTTP handlers.
rule.rationale: Default current_thread masks Send/Sync bugs that appear only under multi-thread runtimes.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
